events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess a panelboard installation with double-tapped breakers (non-listed). The work is rough-complete. We need to decide compliance. Double-tapped breakers refer to two conductors under one terminal where the breaker is not listed for two conductors. This is a violation of NEC 110.14(A) (terminals) and manufacturer instructions. It's a common defect. So decision: fail. Risk: medium? Could be high if overloaded, but generally medium. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 90%? Panelboard installed but makeup not complete? Rough-complete suggests it's installed but not yet closed out. I'll estimate 85% complete. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Double-tapped breakers (non-listed) observed." Actions: "Replace breakers with listed ones for two conductors or add a pigtail to a single conductor per terminal." Rationale: "NEC 110.14(A) requires terminals to be used per listing; double-tapping a non-listed breaker violates this." References: ["NEC 110.14(A)", "NEC 408.41 (maybe)", "manufacturer instructions"]. I'll include NEC 110.14(A) and 408.41? 408.41 is about panelboard circuit directories, not directly. 110.14(A) is the main one. Also 110.3(B) for listing and labeling. So references: ["NEC 110.14(A)", "NEC 110.3(B)"]. I'll output JSON.

  "rationale": "NEC 110.14(A) requires terminals to be used per their listing; double-tapping a breaker not listed for two conductors violates this and manufacturer instructions.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14(A)", "NEC 110.3(B)"]
}
